title: Hermaphrodite Angels
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 02, 2011 4:30 pm Fazmotron wrote: Furthermore, we don't know if Lilith even has ovaries, or a womb

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Fri Dec 02, 2011 8:35 pm ^ I've always suspected this.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Fri Dec 02, 2011 10:14 pm Reichu wrote: According to the genome scan in EoE, Lilith appears to be a hermaphrodite.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 02, 2011 10:51 pm All we really know is that the genome analyses for Shamshel, Gaghiel, and Lilith have a double set of sex chromosomes ( see here ; Y and X are visible at the lower-right corner of the first chromosome set), probably as a biologically naive way of telling anyone who bothers to look, "these beings are both sexes at once". Can probably extrapolate that this applies to all Angels, both seeds, and the Evas, too. The exact details of the hermaphroditism are debatable, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Dec 02, 2011 11:14 pm Reichu wrote: All we really know is that the genome analyses for Shamshel, Gaghiel, and Lilith have a double set of sex chromosomes ( see here ; Y and X are visible at the lower-right corner of the first chromosome set), probably as a biologically naive way of telling anyone who bothers to look, "these beings are both sexes at once". Can probably extrapolate that this applies to all Angels, both seeds, and the Evas, too. The exact details of the hermaphroditism are debatable, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 02, 2011 11:57 pm A single set of Angel (or Lilith; graphic is the same) chromosomes possesses both a Y and an X. Look at the bottom row: 19, 20, 21, 22, Y, X. This probably means that the other set is "...22, Y, X" as well. Hence "double set of sex chromosomes". Biologically naive, but nonetheless sends the message "both sexes at once" loud and clear.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Dec 03, 2011 4:40 am Reichu wrote: A single set of Angel (or Lilith; graphic is the same) chromosomes possesses both a Y and an X. Look at the bottom row: 19, 20, 21, 22, Y, X. This probably means that the other set is "...22, Y, X" as well. Hence "double set of sex chromosomes". Biologically naive, but nonetheless sends the message "both sexes at once" loud and clear.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Dec 03, 2011 12:56 pm Bagheera wrote: Right, I get that. My point is that we're not looking at two sets of chromosomes there.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Sat Dec 03, 2011 3:30 pm Reichu wrote: According to the genome scan in EoE, Lilith appears to be a hermaphrodite.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Dec 03, 2011 3:39 pm KnightmareX13 wrote: could you provide a link to this image

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Dec 03, 2011 6:49 pm Reichu wrote: ...Unless you have another idea as to what both a Y and an X chromosome are doing in a single set, as opposed to being distributed across two.

[]:             Quote Post by SaVaGe » Sat Dec 03, 2011 6:56 pm Of course Lilith isn't completely a girl. Where are her boobies! This actually explains a lot about Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Dec 03, 2011 7:02 pm @Bagheera: So you're saying I've abandoned my dearest fanon and thrown myself out on a limb here all for nothing? Well, shucks. And here I'd thought I'd resolved the problem of the Eva prostates and the Adam mother/father dilemma in an internally consistent manner... @SaVaGe: Secondary sexual characteristics like fatty breasts or overindulgently huge trains of feathers are called "secondary" for a reason.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Dec 03, 2011 7:09 pm Reichu wrote: @Bagheera: So you're saying I've abandoned my dearest fanon and thrown myself out on a limb here all for nothing? Well, shucks. And here I'd thought I'd resolved the problem of the Eva prostates and the Adam mother/father dilemma in an internally consistent manner...

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Sat Dec 03, 2011 7:32 pm I was always wondering how the hell a giant ball of quantum energy could be classified based on gender. This discovery now makes that irrelevant.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Dec 03, 2011 7:53 pm Trajan wrote: I was always wondering how the hell a giant ball of quantum energy could be classified based on gender.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Dec 03, 2011 8:05 pm Reichu wrote: Bagheera does make a good point about the chromosome chart likely being taken from a unisex layout of all human chromosomes, which seems to render the data inconclusive. And I myself point out that the Lilith chart -- upon closer analysis -- is an incoherent mishmash. Interpret with caution, everyone.

[]:             Quote Post by SaVaGe » Sat Dec 03, 2011 8:13 pm Reichu wrote: @SaVaGe: Secondary sexual characteristics like fatty breasts or overindulgently huge trains of feathers are called "secondary" for a reason.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Dec 04, 2011 11:05 am And here I thought Angels being hermaphroditic was damn intuitive. :P

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Fri Dec 02, 2011 8:35 pm ^ I've always suspected this.
